findings.evidence: PicZeroQuotient.lean:272 defines `degreeZeroQuotientEquivUnweightedPicZero` as `degreeZeroQuotientEquivWeightedDegreeZeroOne.trans (weightedDegreeZeroQuotientEquivPicZero (fun _ => 1) h)`; simp lemmas `degreeZeroQuotientEquivWeightedDegreeZeroOne_(symm_)mk` (PicZeroQuotient.lean:259,265) already provide the transport; AbelJacobiSum.lean:251 establishes the derive-from-weighted pattern this file abandons.
findings.fix: Add a bridge lemma `unweightedAbelJacobiQuotientClass x₀ D = degreeZeroQuotientEquivWeightedDegreeZeroOne.symm (weightedAbelJacobiQuotientClass (fun _ => 1) rfl D)` and derive the duplicated unweighted lemmas from their weighted counterparts, mirroring `unweightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass_eq_weighted` (AbelJacobiSum.lean:251).
findings.issue: The whole unweighted section (unweightedAbelJacobiDegreeZeroDivisor / unweightedAbelJacobiQuotientClass and lemmas, lines 234–380) re-proves the weighted API specialized to w = fun _ => 1 by hand (map_add', _eq_sum by full induction, _ofPoint_base, _principalDivisor, _eq_iff_divisorClass, _eq_iff_linearlyEquivalent). The general (weighted) result is already proven above and the unweighted quotient is the w=1 special case connected by an existing transport equiv.

summary: The unweighted quotient-class section duplicates the weighted API for the special case w = fun _ => 1 instead of deriving it through the existing transport equiv, diverging from the surrounding library's derive-from-weighted convention.
